what: Summarize CH₄, N₂O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
title: Summarize CH₄, N₂O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: Calculates summary CH₄ N₂O emissions (direct indirect) land use, grouped code, animal type, subtype, zone. Optionally allows aggregation general level.

title: Summarize CH₄, N₂O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
code: generate_impact_assessment(   group = NULL,   zone = NULL,   animal = NULL,   type = NULL,   saveoutput = TRUE,   group_by_identification = TRUE )

title: Summarize CH₄, N₂O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: group Character/Numeric vector (optional). Groups filter. Default `NULL`. zone Character vector (optional). Zones filter. Default `NULL`. animal Character string (optional). Livestock type (`animal_type`). Default `NULL`. type Character string (optional). Livestock subtype (`animal_subtype`). Default `NULL`. saveoutput Logical. `TRUE`, saves result `"output/generate_impact_assessment.csv"`. Default `TRUE`. group_by_identification Logical. `TRUE`, returns results `identification` level. `FALSE`, aggregates `identification`s.

title: Summarize CH₄, N₂O Emissions, and Land Use — generate_impact_assessment
text: `tibble` columns emission type land use.

title: Package Function Reference
text: Calculates Methane (CH4CH_4) emissions Enteric Fermentation using IPCC Tier 2 methodology. calculation depends heavily animal’s Gross Energy (GE) intake Methane Conversion Factor (YmY_m), varies diet quality.

text: Calculates Methane (CH4CH_4) emissions Manure Management systems. Uses VS value along management system factors (B0B_0, MCFMCF) estimate total CH4CH_4 output.

text: Calculates Nitrogen (NN) Excretion based Nitrogen intake (derived Crude Protein, CP) minus Nitrogen retention (growth/milk). estimates Direct Nitrous Oxide (N2ON_2O) emissions excreted nitrogen.

title: Introduction to herdr
text: herdr R package designed calculate greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions land use (LU) associated livestock production. Currently supported animals: - Cattle - Goat - Sheep

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr
title: Introduction to herdr
text: package offers specific functions model primary sources environmental impact livestock based detailed Energy Balance model. accounts energy requirements maintenance, growth, lactation, pregnancy, work, wool production.

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr > What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Enteric fermentation: Emissions produced digestive process based Gross Energy intake diet digestibility. Manure management: Emissions derived Volatile Solids (VSVS) decay, adjusting specific conditions (MCF) diverse management systems (e.g., liquid lagoons, solid storage, pasture, composting).

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr > What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Direct: Emissions manure storage/treatment systems. Indirect: Emissions via nitrogen volatilization (NH3NH_3, NOxNO_x) leaching water systems.

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr > What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Calculates land area (m2m^2) required produce feed, linking animal intake directly crop yields.

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr > What the Package Calculates
title: Introduction to herdr
text: package designed handle multiple Groups (e.g., farms, countries) Zones (e.g., climatic regions) simultaneously, allowing comparative analysis within single dataset.

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr
title: Introduction to herdr
text: emission calculations within herdr adhere Tier 2 methods established Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change (IPCC). Specifically, use guidelines 2019 Refinement 2006 IPCC Guidelines National Greenhouse Gas Inventories: “Volume 4: Agriculture, Forestry Land Use, Chapter 10: EMISSIONS LIVESTOCK MANURE MANAGEMENT”

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Diet composition primary driver results. functions use nutritional data (Energy, Protein, NDF) determine key parameters like Methane Conversion Factor (YmY_m) Nitrogen Excretion (NexN_{ex}). Automatic Validation: calculating, herdr automatically checks consistency census (population) diet files, ensuring every animal defined corresponding nutritional profile.

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr
title: Introduction to herdr
text: Install package directly GitHub using remotes: Load package explore default datasets:
code: # Instalar 'remotes' si es necesario if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {     install.packages("remotes") } # Instalar el paquete herdr remotes::install_github("JuanCBM99/Animal_GEI_LU") library(herdr)

previous_headings: Introduction to herdr
title: Introduction to herdr
text: familiarizing package, move Example Data vignette detailed instructions : Loading exploring datasets Using data templates Preparing data calculations

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Net Energy Maintenance (NEm) energy required maintain animal’s energy balance. Formula:NEm = Cfi * Weight^0.75  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Net Energy Activity (NEa) represents energy used grazing, walking, animal activities. Cattle Buffalo NEa = Ca * NEm  (MJ/day) Sheep Goats NEa = Ca * Weight  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Net Energy Growth (NEg) energy tissue deposition. Cattle Buffalo NEg = BW^0.75 * (1.097 / 22.02) * (WG / (C * MW))^2  (MJ/day) Sheep Goats NEg = (WGlamb / 365) * (+ b * ((BWi + BWf)/2))  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required milk production: Cattle Buffalo NEl = Milk * (1.47 + 0.40 * Fat)  (MJ/day) Sheep Goats NEl = Milk * EV_milk  (MJ/day)

what: V. Net Energy for Work and Wool Production
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Work (NEwork) draft animals NEwork = 0.10 * NEm * Hours (MJ/day) Wool (NEwool) sheep/goats NEwool = Pr_wool * EV_wool / 365  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Energy required gestation: NEp = C_pregnancy * NEm  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Gross Energy (GE) total daily energy intake required cover Net Energy needs: GE = [(NE_m + NE_a + NE_l + NEwork + NEp)/R_EM + (NEg + NEwool)/R_EG] / DE  (MJ/day)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Tier 1 Structure Tier 2 EF calculation: methane emissions depend population diet-specific emission factor: E_CH4 = Σ (N_animals * EF)  (Gg CH₄/year) EF = (GE * Ym / 100 * 365) / 55.65  (kg CH₄/head/year)

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatile Solids (VS) excretion: VS = (GE / 18.45) * [(1 - DE/100) + (UE * GE / GE)] * (1 - ASH)  (kg/day) Manure CH₄ Emission Factor: EF_CH4_manure = (VS * B0 * 365 / 100) * 0.67 * Σ(AWMS * MCF)  (kg CH₄/year)

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Nintake = GE / 18.45 * (6.25 / 100) * CP%  (kg N/day) Nitrogen retention: Cattle/Buffalo: Nretention = [(NEg + Milk * PR) * 7.03 / (1000 * 6.38)] - [Milk * PR * 6.25 / 100]  (g N/day) Sheep/Goats: Nretention_frac = 0.1 (fraction)Nex = Nintake * (1 - Nretention_frac) * 365  (kg N/year)

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: N2O_direct = 44/28 * Σ [N_animals * Nex * AWMS * EF3]  (kg N2O/year)

previous_headings: X. N₂O Module: Nitrogen-Based Emissions
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Volatilization: N_volatilization = Σ [ (N_animals * Nex + N_codigestate) * fraction_managed * frac_gas ]N2O_volatilization = 44/28 * N_volatilization * EF4 Leaching / Runoff: N_leaching = Σ [ (N_animals * Nex + N_codigestate) * fraction_managed * frac_leach ]N2O_leaching = 44/28 * N_leaching * EF5

title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: Land Use Module calculates agricultural land required support animal category based diet composition crop yields. provides land use per animal total land use population.

previous_headings: XI. Land Use Module
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: calculation requires following datasets:

previous_headings: XI. Land Use Module
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: ingredient diet, weighted consumption calculated: consumption_kg = dm_ingested_total * ingredient_share * share_weight / 10000 (kg) land use per ingredient calculated using crop yields: land_use_m2_per_unit = ifelse(dry_matter_yield > 0, (consumption_kg / dry_matter_yield) * 10000, 0) (m²/animal)

previous_headings: XI. Land Use Module
what: III. Total Land Use per Animal
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: total land use per animal obtained summing land required ingredients: Land_use_per_animal = sum(land_use_m2_per_unit) (m²/animal)

previous_headings: XI. Land Use Module
what: IV. Total Land Use for the Population
title: Theoretical Basis: IPCC Tier 2 Methodology
text: total land required livestock category : Land_use_total = Land_use_per_animal * population (m²)

what: ⚙️ Step 4: Run the Model with Your Own Data
title: Workflow Guide: Step-by-Step
code: # Run the model using the customised data inside /user_data resultados_personalizados <- generate_impact_assessment(   saveoutput = TRUE,   group_by_identification = TRUE )  # Example: filter results by zone (if census.csv includes zones) resultados_zona_A <- generate_impact_assessment(zone = "Zone_A")
